preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial stop [p] to its voiced counterpart [b] is a classic example of voicing lenition. It most frequently occurs in intervocalic positions where the stop adopts the voicing of the surrounding vowels.

examples.note: Tamil intervocalic voicing: /p/ → [b] between vowels. E.g., Tamil 'appan' (father) → [ambˈan]; 'paḷḷam' → medial [b] allophone. Systematic Dravidian alternation.
Intervocalic voicing of the labial stop.
Soft mutation (treiglad meddal) voices the initial voiceless stop.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
